title: packera clevelandii
intro: Packera clevelandii, also known as Cleveland's ragwort or Cleveland's groundsel, is a perennial herbaceous plant in the dandelion family that is native to the western United States. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to aid in digestion and relieve symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) due to its prebiotic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Packera clevelandii include reducing inflammation, improving gut health, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
